posts.text:
The World Cup does not start with the opening whistle. It starts with the last difficult phone call.

All 48 final squads are set to be confirmed tomorrow. Track every team and make your early call:

posts.text:

Follow the fixtures and team routes:

posts.text:
10 days to go.

Mexico City will not just host the opener. It will set the mood for the first 48-team World Cup.

Build your match plan:

posts.altText: Fans walk near a Mexico City football stadium as a vendor arranges plain scarves.

posts.title: World Cup 2026 Final Squads: The Deadline-Day Checklist
posts.text: The final World Cup squads are set to be confirmed on June 2. The interesting question is not only who made the cut, but what each coach chose to sacrifice: depth, experience or a wildcard. Save this checklist and compare all 48 teams with WorldCupDex.
posts.altText: A top-down football squad planning desk with shirt magnets, a checklist and tactics markers.

posts.title: How to Follow Your Team Through World Cup 2026
posts.text: With 48 teams and 104 matches, trying to follow everything is a fast route to missing the match you care about. Start with one team: fixtures, local kickoff times, stadiums and possible knockout paths. WorldCupDex keeps the route practical.
posts.altText: A cafe table with a North America route map, calendar reminders, scarf and coffee.

posts.title: The First 48-Team World Cup: A Fan's Simple Planning Guide
posts.text: The 2026 World Cup is bigger: 48 teams, 104 matches and three host countries. Bigger is not automatically better for fans unless the schedule becomes personal. Save the overview, pick your team route and keep the matches that matter close.
posts.altText: A fan pins a marker on a planning board filled with plain football shirt cutouts.

posts.text:
Tomorrow, the squad lists become official.

That sentence sounds tidy. Selection day is not.

For every obvious starter, there is a player refreshing a phone. For every exciting wildcard, there is a coach choosing which kind of risk to accept. The World Cup begins long before the first whistle. It begins with 48 versions of the same difficult decision.

Explore the teams and make your early predictions through the link in bio.

#WorldCup2026 #Football #Soccer #WorldCupSquads #WorldCupDex
posts.altText: An anonymous footballer waits on a training-facility bench while holding a phone.

posts.text:
10 days until Mexico City opens the door.

The first 48-team World Cup will be huge: 104 matches across three countries. But the best way to enjoy it may be smaller. Pick your team. Mark your nights. Know the route. Let the tournament grow one match at a time.

WorldCupDex is built for that second-screen ritual. Fixtures, teams and predictions are waiting through the link in bio.

#WorldCup2026 #MexicoCity #Football #Soccer #WorldCupDex
posts.altText: Friends prepare a football match-night table on a Mexico City rooftop at sunset.
